protein_name: Acts as a repressor in the regulation of translation initiation of poly(A)-containing mRNAs. Its inhibitory activity on translation is mediated via its action on PABPC1. Displaces the interaction of PABPC1 with poly(A) RNA and competes with PAIP1 for binding to PABPC1. Its association with PABPC1 results in disruption of the cytoplasmic poly(A) RNP structure organization. Belongs to the PAIP2 family. Expressed at highest level in testis, but also abundant in brain, cervix, lung, ovary, placenta, adipose tissue, thymus and thyroid. Note: This description may include information from UniProtKB.

raw_fields.Molecular Function: mRNA binding; mRNA regulatory element binding translation repressor activity; protein binding; translation repressor activity
raw_fields.Biological Process: negative regulation of translation; negative regulation of translational initiation; regulation of translation
